general_notes: This table contains extracted claims with citations that have been validated. The ID columns (Claim ID, Statement, Context, Reference) are already filled with the extracted claims. The RESEARCH columns (Support Level, Confidence, Validation Notes) should be populated through validation to verify how well each reference supports its claim.

search_groups.description: Claim identifiers and extracted text used for context - already populated
Validation of how well the reference supports the claim using web search to access cited sources

examples: The Earth's average temperature has increased by 1.1°C since pre-industrial times
The human brain contains approximately 86 billion neurons
validation_targets.notes: The claim that needs to be verified against the provided reference

validation_targets.description: Additional context surrounding the claim from the original text
validation_targets.notes: Provides context for understanding the claim's intended meaning

validation_targets.description: The citation or source provided to support the claim
examples: Herculano-Houzel, S. (2009). The human brain in numbers. Frontiers in Human Neuroscience, 3, 31
validation_targets.notes: The source that should support the claim - may be a URL, academic citation, or source description

validation_targets.description: Assessment of how well the reference supports the claim
validation_targets.format: String (strongly_supported, supported, partially_supported, unclear, contradicted, or inaccessible)
validation_targets.notes: Validate by accessing the reference and comparing to the claim. Return one of: strongly_supported (claim matches reference exactly), supported (claim is backed by reference with minor differences), partially_supported (reference supports some but not all of claim), unclear (reference is ambiguous or unclear), contradicted (reference contradicts the claim), inaccessible (reference cannot be accessed or found)

validation_targets.description: Confidence score for the support level assessment
validation_targets.notes: 0.0-1.0 scale indicating confidence in the support level assessment. Higher values indicate greater certainty about the assessment.

validation_targets.description: Detailed explanation of the validation findings
examples: Reference directly states the claim with exact temperature value. Source is authoritative (IPCC AR6).
Reference confirms Python was released in 1991 by Guido van Rossum. Official documentation source.
Reference provides different estimate (85 billion) but within margin of error. Peer-reviewed neuroscience paper.
Reference URL returns 404 error. Unable to verify claim.
validation_targets.notes: Provide: (1) What the reference says about the claim, (2) Key differences if any, (3) Source reliability assessment. Keep concise but informative.

storage_metadata.description: Static validation configuration for reference checking. Used to validate claims against their provided citations. The ID columns are populated during extraction, and the RESEARCH columns are filled during validation.
